statement: Terminal failure must be represented explicitly on target rows instead of inferred from `!has_pending && max_attempts >= cap`.
rationale: The current inference leaks successful last-attempt targets into failure summaries because the schema lacks an explicit resolution field.
keyAssumptions: Target rows can cheaply persist resolution state without harming retry logic.
validationCriteria: Completed successful targets are no longer counted as terminal failures.
Codex and lab can distinguish historical retries from exhausted failures.
critiquePoints: The migration must preserve auto-heal behavior for genuinely retryable targets.
questions: Which explicit enum best fits: pending|retryable|exhausted|succeeded or a richer split?
